publication.showAs: Official Journal of the European Union, L series, entry 2024/1689, 12 July 2024
publication.name: oj-l

eventRef.showAs: Issue published online and made authentic by Publications Office of the EU

TLCOrganization.showAs: Publications Office of the European Union (OP, Luxembourg)

proprietary.akndiff:researchNotes:
Friction log for EU OJ L_2024/1689 — Phase 1.
1. NEW OJ FORMAT 2023+ → ONE-DOC PER ENTRY. Since 1 October 2023, the EU Official Journal has replaced the prior "L 277/p. 1" format (numbered issues containing many dispositions) with per-act unique IDs (L_YYYY/N). Each Regulation/Directive has its own OJ "entry" effectively. JournalTable's contract assumes one issue contains many dispositions — like ES BOE núm. 87 with 50+ dispositions of varying rangos. For EU post-2023, the journal is degenerate: 1 entry = 1 disposition. The schema technically loads it but the relationship "documents in this issue" returns N=1, which makes the journal model less useful cross-country.
2. NO PAGE NUMBERS, NO CUMULATIVE INDEX. Pre-2023 OJ L had page numbers per issue ("L 277, p. 1") and a cumulative table of contents per issue. Post-2023, no pages, no per-issue index; each entry is its own URL. The journal type's issueNumber/issuedAt fields work fine but lose semantic value (issueNumber is "L_2024/1689" which is the SAME as the act's OJ reference — no separate journal identity).
3. PUBLICATIONS OFFICE OF THE EU AS PUBLISHER. JournalTable.publisher is free text; we use "Publications Office of the European Union". No friction.
4. SCOPE: 'eu' VALUE. JournalTable.scope is enum-like (national/regional/eu/municipal). 'eu' fits. No friction.
5. MULTILINGUAL OFFICIAL VERSION. The OJ publishes the act simultaneously in all 24 EU official languages, all equally authentic. Schema's language field on DocumentTable is single- valued (we picked 'eng'). The 24-language equal-authenticity is a multilingual stretch beyond ES (which has co-official catalan/ gallego as separate consolidated PDFs). Cross-country friction amplification.
6. SPLIT JOURNAL SERIES. EU OJ has L (Legislation), C (Communication - non-binding), C-A (calls/recruitment), S (tenders, separate database). Each act is published in its appropriate series. Schema's scope field doesn't model series-within-publisher; we encode L vs C in akndiff:journalIssueNumber prefix only.

longTitle.p: Official Journal of the European Union — L series — entry 2024/1689 of 12 July 2024.

body.p: This OJ entry contains the publication of Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 13 June 2024 laying down harmonised rules on artificial intelligence and amending Regulations (EC) No 300/2008, (EU) No 167/2013, (EU) No 168/2013, (EU) 2018/858, (EU) 2018/1139 and (EU) 2019/2144 and Directives 2014/90/EU, (EU) 2016/797 and (EU) 2020/1828 (Artificial Intelligence Act). (Text with EEA relevance.)
Per the post-2023 OJ format reform, this is a per-act unique entry: one OJ "issue" = one act. The act text occupies the entirety of this entry, in all 24 EU official languages.
